# Transportation Impact Brief — Dana (PERSONA-009)
**Local TV News Producer | Soundbyte Hunter**

---

## Executive Summary

South Portland's administration recommended a school reconfiguration plan — Option A — without first modeling its transportation costs. At the March 30, 2026 board meeting, the Director of Operations confirmed that route planning is "underway with a partner" but will not be complete before the board votes. Independent analysis shows Option A carries $946,000 to $1.68 million in total annual fiscal exposure — potentially exceeding the budget savings it is meant to deliver by 12%. That is the contradiction at the center of this story.

---

## Key Finding

**The district confirmed it has 20 bus drivers. Its recommended plan requires 30.**

That 10-to-13 driver shortfall was confirmed at the March 30 meeting by Director of Operations Mike Natalie — after the SEA staffing cut of 14% had already been decided. No route modeling existed at the time of that decision. Even the lowest-impact option on the table (Option B) requires 24 drivers, leaving a shortfall of 4 to 7 under current staffing.

*This is your lede.* The budget math was done before the bus math.

---

## Configuration Comparison

| Metric | Option A *(Recommended)* | Option B | Variant C |
|---|---|---|---|
| Drivers needed | 30 | 24 | 29 |
| Confirmed drivers available | 17–20 | 17–20 | 17–20 |
| **Driver shortfall** | **10–13** | **4–7** | **9–12** |
| Families split across buildings | 139–169 | 0 | 123–150 |
| Families facing childcare gap | 42–144 | 0 | 37–128 |
| Family-borne care cost (annual) | $144K–$804K | $0 | $127K–$714K |
| District annual cost | $803K–$876K | $30K–$80K | $670K–$736K |
| **Total fiscal exposure (annual)** | **$946K–$1.68M** | **$30K–$80K** | **$797K–$1.45M** |
| As % of claimed savings | 43%–112% | 1.4%–5.3% | 36%–97% |

Option B eliminates both the split-family problem and the childcare cost entirely. It still has a driver shortfall — but at 4–7 missing drivers, it is the only configuration where the gap is potentially closeable without structural changes to the SEA contract.

---

## What's Missing

The district has not produced — and has confirmed it will not produce before the vote — the following:

- **A route map for any configuration.** No origin-destination data, no route counts, no modeled run times. The administration stated planning is "underway with a partner" but gave no completion date.
- **A driver recruitment or retention plan.** The 14% SEA cut reduces the driver pool. No plan exists on record to offset that reduction before fall 2026.
- **A fourth-tier bell schedule cost estimate.** Option A may require a fourth bus tier due to grade-band routing. The current driver idle window (9:30 AM–1:30 PM) leaves no margin for a fourth tier without overtime — which is uncosted.
- **McKinney-Vento individual routing costs.** The analysis estimates $54K–$128K annually for Option A based on per-pupil averages; the district has not disclosed which students are MV-eligible or what their actual transport arrangements cost.
- **Before/after care capacity by school.** After-care is confirmed full at four of five elementary schools (only Kaler, the closing building, has openings). The district has not modeled where displaced families will absorb care costs.

---

## Limitations

This analysis is built from public meeting testimony, budget documents, and the March 30, 2026 board meeting record. It does not have access to:

- The district's internal route modeling (if any exists)
- Actual SEA driver contract terms or overtime rates
- Student address data needed for precise MV or split-family counts
- Real before/after care enrollment figures by school

The driver counts (17–20 post-cut) and route requirements (24–30 depending on configuration) are derived from testimony and confirmed SEA staffing figures, not from an internal district model. The care-gap cost range ($144K–$804K for Option A) reflects a range of childcare rate assumptions — the midpoint is the most defensible single figure for broadcast use.

---

## Invitation to Improve

The district can narrow every range in this brief with data it already holds. Specifically:

- **Route planning output** from the transportation consultant (timeline and draft deliverables)
- **MV-eligible student count** and current transport arrangements by building
- **Before/after care enrollment and waitlist** data by school, by program
- **SEA driver contract** — base pay, overtime rate, and minimum hours — to cost the shortfall directly

Any of these datasets, provided before the vote, would materially sharpen the fiscal picture for the public. This brief will be revised to reflect district-supplied figures on receipt.

---

*Sources: SPEC-060 through SPEC-065; Transportation Claims Catalog (TC-001 through TC-027); March 30, 2026 board meeting testimony, Director of Operations Mike Natalie.*
